subject: English / First Flight
overall_learning_objectives: Analyze the conflict between Amanda and her parent
Interpret the themes of freedom, control, and adolescence
Examine the use of imagery, symbolism, and repetition
Differentiate between tone in reality and imagination
Develop analytical and reflective writing skills

lesson_plan.title: Introduction and Contextual Understanding
topics_covered: Introduction to themes of freedom and parental control
teaching_strategy: Brief introduction to the poet and her focus on teenage issues
Encourage students to reflect on balanced parenting
student_activities: Group discussion on freedom vs discipline
assessment: Oral Q&A on poet and expected themes

lesson_plan.title: Detailed Reading and Speaker Analysis
topics_covered: Identification of parent’s voice vs Amanda’s imagination
student_activities: Create two columns: Reality vs Imagination

lesson_plan.title: Themes and Symbolism
topics_covered: Symbolism of mermaid, orphan, and Rapunzel
teaching_strategy: Explain symbolic meanings of each role
Discuss how fantasy reflects emotional needs
Guide students in textual evidence identification
student_activities: Write symbolic meaning of each imagined role
Support answers with lines from the poem

lesson_plan.title: Literary Devices and Structural Analysis
teaching_strategy: Analyze impact of structure on theme
student_activities: Identify examples of repetition and imagery
Explain their effect in short notes
assessment: Short written explanation of structural effect

lesson_plan.title: Critical Reflection and Creative Response
student_activities: Write a diary entry from Amanda’s perspective (120 words)
Compose a short analytical paragraph on the poem’s message
assessment: Evaluate diary entry for emotional depth
Assess analytical paragraph for clarity and textual support

teacher_tip: Encourage students to think beyond surface meaning. Guide them to understand psychological conflict and symbolic depth while maintaining a supportive classroom environment.
